programme.title: Eye See You
programme.desc: Recovering from the psychological effects of witnessing a brutal crime, FBI Agent Jake Malloy (Sylvester Stallone) checks into a rehabilitation clinic for law enforcement officers in the remote wilderness of Wyoming during the dead of winter. He soon finds himself helping other patients as a serial killer begins killing them one by one.

programme.title: Thrill Seekers
programme.desc: When intrepid reporter Tom Merrick discovers that the same man appears in photographs of major disasters over a hundred-year period, he embarks on an incredible action-filled race against time to prevent a series of new disasters.

programme.title: Gang of Roses
programme.desc: A gang of gold-hunting outlaws, headed by the ruthless Left Eye Watkins (Bobby Brown), takes over the peaceful town of Flatridge, tearing the sleepy outpost to shreds as they search for riches. They murder a can-can girl, who is the sister of Rachel (Monica Calhoun), the onetime leader of a notorious all-girl gang. Rachel then rounds up her four old associates, and they seek revenge for her sister. Using teamwork and a sassy attitude, Rachel's gang goes up against Watkins' tough crew.

programme.title: Jack's Back [Broadcast Edit]
programme.desc: In a sickening coincidence -- or a sinister homage to actual crimes -- women are being murdered in Los Angeles 100 years after Jack the Ripper terrorized London. Police think John Westford, a young doctor, is the murderer, and when he's found hanging from a noose, authorities believe his apparent suicide is a tacit admission of guilt. But his twin brother, Rick (also Spader), insists John was killed, and, when it appears Rick is right, the investigation takes a stunning turn.

programme.title: Blind Horizon [Broadcast Edit]
programme.desc: Found in the New Mexico desert with a head wound and total amnesia, Frank Kavanaugh (Val Kilmer) is brought to a hospital. When he recovers some of his memory, he begins raving about an upcoming assassination attempt on the president. Despite the man's unstable state, Sheriff Jack Kolb (Sam Shepard) is troubled by Frank's story. Discovering the truth grows more complicated when Chloe Richards (Neve Campbell) arrives and claims to be Frank's fiancée, even though he has no memory of her.

programme.title: Arlington Road
programme.desc: Man begins to suspect his neighbors are not what they appear to be and their secrets could be deadly

programme.title: Jobs
programme.desc: The story of Steve Jobs' ascension from college dropout into one of the most revered creative entrepreneurs of the 20th century

programme.title: Silent Hill Revelation
programme.desc: When her adoptive father disappears, Sharon Da Silva is drawn into a strange and terrifying alternate reality that holds answers to the horrific nightmares that have plagued her since childhood.

programme.title: First Knight
programme.desc: A new vision of Camelot, filled with breathtaking battles, heart-pounding courage and love that brought a kingdom to its knees.

programme.title: Big Fish
programme.desc: The story revolves around a dying father (played by Finney, and McGregor as a young man) and his son (Crudup). Trying to learn more about his dad by piecing together the stories he has gathered over the years, the son winds up re-creating his father s elusive life in a series of legends and myths inspired by the few facts he knows. Through these tales, the son begins to understand his father s great feats and his great failings.

programme.title: Chances Are
programme.desc: A reincarnated soul (Robert Downey, Jr.) falls in love with his widow (Cybill Shepherd) 25 years after his death.

programme.title: My Mom's New Boyfriend

programme.title: The Guns Of Navarone
programme.desc: In this WWII spectacle, British intelligence learns that two enormous guns have been installed on the Aegean island of Navarone. The long-range field pieces are capable of destroying any British fleet trying to sail to Kheros, near Turkey, where a large British force is facing annihilation unless it is evacuated. It s the job of Captain Mallory and a handful of men to land secretly on Navarone and dismantle the guns.

programme.desc: Boston Blackie persuades the Governor to release some prisoners to him for war work in a defense plant. Some of them have larceny and not patriotism in mind. Stars Chester Morris, Eric Rolf, Jeanne Bates, Richard Lane, George E. Stone, Lloyd Corrigan and Walter Sande.

programme.desc: With her wedding days away, Kara agrees to meet her ex, Malik, for what she swears is just one last goodbye. But Malik isn’t looking for closure—he wants her back, and he’s not taking no for an answer. What starts as a simple meetup at a hotel turns into a dangerous game of obsession and control. As Kara’s fiancé Jordan uncovers the truth with help from her maid of honor, time’s running out.

programme.title: H.P. Lovecraft'S The Deep Ones
programme.desc: A married couple rents a beach side Airbnb only to be surrounded by peculiar neighbors and occurrences. They soon discover to be in the grips of a mysterious cult and their ancient sea god.

programme.title: White Zombie
programme.desc: Bela Lugosi gave one of his most classic characterizations as the voodoo master in this minor classic of terror. Here, the horror and supernatural aspects of the plot were not the result of mans imitation of dread superstition. Here, the zombies are true creatures of the dead, under the control of zombie-master Lugosi, a delicious evil sort who weaves his web of evil to ensnare an innocent woman and the plantation owner who covets her for himself, even though she loves another man. The film has been called a terror fairy tale with its heroine clothed in flowing white and Lugosis fiend cloaked in darkness. Enjoyable, at times chilling, White Zombie, despite some moments of over-melodramatic acting, is a fascinating exercise in the macabre, containing some truly classic moments in the genre of the horror film.

programme.title: A Dark Song
programme.desc: A heartbroken woman and recluse occultist perform an ancient, dark ritual to bring her child back to life - but missteps have consequences and unknown evils are unleashed.

programme.desc: A platoon of American soldiers finds something more sinister than war behind enemy lines. Then, a man with a passion for found objects finds something particularly odd: a finger ... and it's growing.

programme.desc: "Elly Races Jetherine" - When Sonny Drysdale promises to give Elly May a ring, Granny thinks he's going to propose and becomes a matchmaker. Cousin Pearl can't stand the idea that Elly May will get married before Jethrine, so she sets to matchmaking Jethrine with Jasper Depew.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Pygmalion and Elly" - Sonny Drysdale decides he needs to be Pygmalion to Elly's Galatea and remake her from a hillbilly into a woman of society, while Granny uses love charms to heat up their relationship.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Elly's First Date" - Mr. Drysdale convinces his self-absorbed stepson Sonny to date Elly May to Mrs. Drysdale's consternation, while Granny and Jed make preparations for Thanksgiving.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jethro Goes to School" - Jed enrolls Jethro at an exclusive Beverly Hills elementary school. Millicent Schuyler-Potts, the proprietor of the school, is aghast when Jed and Jethro show up, convinced this is part of some ghastly hoax. After Jed says that banker Milburn Drysdale is his neighbor, she calls the banker. It turns out that Drysdale's bank holds the mortgage on the school. Schulyer-Potts suddenly warms to the charms of the Clampetts, even if it means accepting Jethro as a fifth grader.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Servants" - Ellie May starts wearing dresses but takes it as an insult when someone says she looks like a city girl. Mr. Drysdale talks Jed into taking in his butler and upstairs girl in a plan to civilize the Clampetts, but they don't quite understand the idea of servants. Miss Hathaway takes Jethro bowling and begins to show an interest in him.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Race for Queen" - Elly May competes in a contest to determine "The Queen of Beverly Hills."

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Lafe Lingers On" - Lafe Crick comes back in another scheme to get rich off the Clampetts' wealth.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Girl From Home" - A father and daughter from back home come to Beverly Hills to meet Jed.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Giant Jackrabbit" - Granny encounters a kangaroo, and thinks she's found a giant jackrabbit, but no one will believe her.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Mr. Drysdale's idea for Christmas presents are ones only fit for coastal California, like diving suits and a boat, and a television set, which Granny thinks is some kind of fancy washing machine.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Get Culture" - The Clampetts decide to get a taste of culture to fit in the high-class society of Beverly Hills.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Elly Needs a Maw" - Mr. Drysdale needs investors for his real estate development project, so he enlists the Widow Fenwick, and aims to cement the deal by hitching her to Jed Clampett, who is looking to find a mother for his daughter, Elly May.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Garden Party" - Mrs. Drysdale has a garden party and 'invites' the Clampetts by mistake. They end up back by their 'cement pond' to handle the 'overflow' from Mrs. Drysdale's party. A gun shot by Granny starts the real party.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Turkey Day" - Elly bonds with the Thanksgiving turkey so the family can't bring themselves to kill it; Mrs Drysdale hires some Indians to take a Thanksgiving picture, which puts Granny on the warpath.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Go Hollywood" - When Jake Clampett tells Jed he's his cousin, he's welcomed into the home and soon has everyone but Jed thinking about and dressing for a role in their next movie.

programme.desc: The 50th of 66 Hopalong Cassidy films finds Hoppy in a vengeful mood. When his good friend Tim Mason is unjustly sent to jail and then killed when trying to escape, Hoppys bitterness gets him drummed out of the Texas Rangers. With the help of his pals California Carson and Jimmy Rogers, he sets out to expose the real culprits and clear Tims name. The usual cast is here, including William Boyd as Hoppy, plus Andy Clyde, Jimmy Rogers, and in one of his earliest roles, Robert Mitchum.

programme.title: High Lonesome
programme.desc: A young drifter is suspected of murder in this stirring production from writer/director Alan Le May (best known for writing The Searchers). John Drew Barrymore stars as Cooncat, a troubled young man who has to steal to survive. With no friends in West Texas, he falls in with some bad customers who involve him in some terrible crimes. The strong cast includes Chill Wills, Jack Elam, and John Archer, with cinematography by Oscar winner W. Howard Greene.

programme.title: Born to Battle
programme.desc: Cyclone Tom Saunders starts a ruckus in a Chinese laundry and has to go before a judge. Unable to pay his fine, he is bailed out by a local cattle rancher and agrees to do 30 days' work for him. It's not too long before he becomes enmeshed in a war between rustlers and outlaws.

programme.title: The Gunman from Bodie
programme.desc: The Rough Riders, a popular group that thrilled audiences in the early 1940s, are after a gang of rustlers in this B-western from Hollywood's Poverty Row. Starring Buck Jones, Tim McCoy, and Raymond Hatton, this movie features everything from lawmen going undercover to the Rough Riders looking after an orphaned baby! Also, be on the lookout for the beautiful Christine McIntyre, who would go on to appear in several of the Three Stooges shorts for Colombia Pictures.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: In 2010, Amberly Snyder’s life changed forever when an accident left her paralyzed from the waist-down. Her comeback story not unlike something you’d see in a movie…in fact it inspired one. Disabled Outdoorsmen USA contacted TTHA and set up this hunt and we were fortunate enough to be able to capture just some of Amberly’s story and her harvest of a beautiful Axis buck at the G2 Ranch in the spring of 2022.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: This spring, our crew was able to dive into some turkey hunting at the G2 ranch with long time TTHA Members Mike Reeber and Dan Marcasol. The night features a high-stakes lineup of JCB Racing, the technical Two Wheel Skills Challenge, and the fan-favorite Freestyle competition where drivers have 75 seconds to push their machines to the limit. Rivalries ignite as four-time champion Weston Anderson and 2025 Entertainer of the Year Fernando Martinez battle for dominance, while local heroes like Brandon and Mikayla Tulachka look to claim victory in front of their hometown crowd. programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: The hosts celebrate their 25th show milestone with guest Chadwick Boyd, who elevates the classic Green Bean Casserole with caramelized onions, fresh beans, and a gruyere drop biscuit topping. While the casserole bakes, the crew debates mashed potato techniques, and Michael shares a hilarious story about how an AI misread his pan wall photo. A fan wins a Caraway pan set.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: A Fan Fridge rescue mission unfolds when a hypothetical Thanksgiving goes wrong. Michael Symon saves the day using Tammy's fridge ingredients to create a savory skillet of chicken thighs, Italian sausage, and sweet peppers. Meanwhile, the hosts share their current TV binges, Michael explains the meanings behind his tattoos, and Clinton sings "Brandy" for a fan. The episode wraps with a giveaway of Carla's measuring bowls.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Guest Danielle Sepsy of The Hungry Gnome joins the kitchen to bake her famous, plush Honey Corn Muffins. As the muffins bake, Clinton and Danielle test Carla and Michael's Long Island accents in a hilarious roleplay game. They also play Chew It or Screw It, share Thanksgiving food disaster stories, and give away a $75 gift card to Danielle's bakery to a fan in Virginia.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Kicking off Thanksgiving week, Clinton Kelly prepares a seasonal Delicata Squash Salad with goat cheese and pepitas. As the crew enjoys the autumn flavors, they discuss Thanksgiving versus Friendsgiving, dive into a comprehensive timeline for holiday prep, and give away a prize to a lucky fan from Oregon.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: It's Thanksgiving Eve, and Carla Hall takes the stress out of holiday cooking with her fast and flavorful Turkey Scallopini topped with a Cranberry Gastrique. The hosts share their best organizational tips, including Michael's famous oven grid and platter sticky notes. The trio also swaps hilarious stories about cooking under pressure and celebrity encounters.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: It's the Friday after Thanksgiving, and Chewed Up is all about transforming leftovers into brand-new masterpieces. Carla Hall whips up a decadent Ice Cream Pecan Pie using leftover pie slices, while Michael Symon cooks a savory Stuffing Frittata. The hosts confess their favorite late-night leftover habits, debate mayonnaise brands, and share wild stories about flying with pets.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: In this Fan Fridge episode, Michael Symon battles a stubborn blender to create a Whipped Cottage Cheese Dip with crispy mortadella chips. The crew embraces the pre-holiday scramble, sharing funny stories about morning commutes and paper cuts.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: During "holiday reset week," Clinton Kelly bakes a festive Stromboli Wreath in a bundt pan. The hosts discuss their vastly different childhoods, play "Holiday Do or Don't," and Michael shares a hilariously horrifying mix-up involving the Hotel Chelsea in New York.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Special guest Liz Symon joins the kitchen to bake her foolproof Orange Olive Oil Cake. While she bakes, Liz and Michael laugh off a viral AI rumor that they divorced, reveal their morning Wordle routines, and recall a disastrous early date involving bad crab stuffed shells.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Guest Amy Sedaris joins the crew for a festive break! Michael leans into his Greek heritage to bake savory Spanakopita triangles, while Clinton and Amy get crafty making adorable "Pinecone Gnomes." The group plays "Chew It or Screw It," debates edible glitter, and shares their most hated foods.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: It's "Holiday Reset Week"! Carla Hall brings a comforting yet lighter meal to the table: Roasted Cauliflower Steaks served with a creamy cauliflower puree and buttery breadcrumbs. The hosts debate the best Christmas movies, Clinton pitches "Bean Week," and the studio briefly loses power!

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly bakes an impressive yet simple "Invisible Apple Cake" loaded with thinly sliced apples, cinnamon, and nutmeg. The crew discusses the dangers of mandolins, Michael gets emotional recalling a surprise restaurant visit, and they debate holiday party etiquette.

programme.title: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes
programme.desc: Dinner Time Live: The Recipes - Mille-Feuille Nabe & Krispy Kouign-Amann - Chris recreates two recipes from Dinner Time Live with David Chang: Mille-Feuille Nabe and "Krispy" Kouign-Amann.

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "Chris Makes Beef & Broccoli" - Who doesn't love broccoli beef (other than vegetarians)??? Lucky for you, Chris Ying woke up with a craving for THAT EXACT DISH. Here he shows you how to make his dead simple version (using the microwave to speed things up). This is some high-quality weeknight cooking, people! Make it for yourself and let us know what you think.

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "Nico de Leon's Inasal Fried Chicken Sandwich" - Chef Nico de Leon shares his recipe for a delicious twice fried Inasal Chicken Sandwich, bringing in Filipino flavors and plenty of pro tips for creating a beautiful sandwich.

programme.title: Cooking School
programme.desc: "Fresh Bok Choy Kimchi with Monica Lee" - The wonderful Monica Lee of Beverly Soon Tofu is back in the kitchen with her daughters to share her recipe for fresh Bok Choy Kimchi. This is an easy way to have a big batch of homemade kimchi in the fridge, or split it in half to use some to make a quick banchan. This recipe and many more are available in Monica's new book, Sohn-mat: Recipes and Flavors of Korean Home Cooking.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave makes an incredibly unlikely dish for him to make: a casserole. Dave has Oscar and Emmy-winning writer/director Cord Jefferson as his guest and makes chicken divan to try to bring him back to his childhood. The duo talk their recent trip to Las Vegas together, Cord's unusual route to where he is today, and their philosophies on failure and success. They talk about maintaining a worldly curiosity, trying new things, and how learning what you don't like can be just as important as learning what you do, then finish with a MOIF about Vegas.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave makes a quick snack that you might see more and more often in the coming years: dried squid. He talks about the rising amount of cephalopods in the ocean, the specific umami and texture of squid, and ponders if it might be coming to more and more grocery store shelves near you. He also answers an Ask Dave about London eating, and general dining strategy when on a short trip in a new place.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave is joined by friend of the show and host of Recipe Club, Chris Ying, and producer (Young) David Meyer. Dave serves a Chinese chicken salad and some leftover jangjorim, then the trio dives into some real "Inappropriate Dinner Conversations" in a new game version that reveals a lot about the three contestants.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave cooks a deceivingly simple dish he had years ago, as served by Chef Mark Ladner. This sugar snap pea dish really impacted Dave and his future, as he tells. He also talks seasonality of ingredients, maturing beyond knowing what you can do into deciding what you should do, and modern art.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave shares what he's learned about living in the moment from his dog Seve, who is sick. He makes his (and, more importantly, Seve's) favorite dog food recipe, while talking about their time together, as well as a bit about those topics we should all talk about more: mortality, loss, and love.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave cooks jangjorim, a Korean braised dish usually served as a side, but that Dave thinks could be an entree and a star. He also discusses how to know when rice is properly washed, as well as the difference between mu radish and daikon (amongst other Korean and Japanese foods). Dave finishes the episode by answering an Ask Dave about who he would want as company for his dream last meal and his answer is...surprising.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave is joined by a friend of the podcast, Noelle, partner at Majordomo Media, as he cooks her a meaningful dish that she did not know he was going to make. They talk about Paris (related to the dish), changes in the global dining scene, and some necessary improvements on the rules of sharing food.

programme.title: Billy & Dom Eat The World
programme.desc: Billy and Dom share their favorite haunts in Los Angeles, including the famous Mel’s Diner in Santa Monica. They take a trip to Sonoma County for a farm-to-table adventure and get a rare opportunity to take a fascinating glimpse at the world’s most advanced lab-grown meat facility in Oakland.

programme.title: Billy & Dom Eat The World
programme.desc: Billy and Dom head to Dom’s hometown of Manchester, the world’s first industrialised city and now a vibrant food capital. They brunch at cult diner Gooey, learn to cook curry at a classic “Rice & Three” café, explore the science behind ice cream cones, and enjoy a special tour of Manchester United’s iconic stadium.

programme.title: Billy & Dom Eat The World
programme.desc: Billy and Dom journey across Costa Rica from the northern Pacific coast to San José. In Nicoya, a renowned Blue Zone, they uncover dietary secrets linked to long, healthy lives. Venturing into the jungle, they work on a coffee plantation and discover why Costa Rica is the world’s leading exporter of pineapples.

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: At the Mercato Centrale in San Lorenzo, one can taste the best food treasures Tuscany has on offer: from the olive oil of the Fiesole hills to the onions of the medieval borough of Certaldo and up to the Natural Reserve of San Marcello with its Tuscan cured meats. Here the simple and genuine tastes are king.

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: An enchanting city, Barcelona is brimming with inspirations left by the greatest of artists. But this charming city has another jewel in its crown - La Boqueria food market. Take a journey through the senses with our lively characters and uncover their gastronomic secrets.

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: Lying in the culinary heart of Vienna like an island, a little world of treats rises in the midst: the Naschmarkt food market. A throng of buyers and curious onlookers meander through the stalls all consumed by the scent of spices, distracted by the smell of sausage, and stunned by the diversity of turnips, tubers, foliage, and fruit on display. The Naschmarkt truly is Vienna's most popular belly.

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: Food markets have a strong tradition in the Netherlands and many citizens count on their long street markets for their fresh food supplies. One of the oldest and most famous markets of the Netherlands is the Albert Cuyp Markt, a street market stretching along the upcoming (and "hip") neighborhood of de Pijp.

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: Placed in the middle of Palermo's ancient streets, it is almost difficult to walk. All around there is a swarm of colors and perfumes. There is everything the Mediterranean island, so rich in agricultural traditions, has to offer. Street food here is a must, including dozens of roasted, salted, pickled, and fried specialties one can count on.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: Our 4 Seasons of Home Ownership breaks a year's worth of chores into 4 simple seasonal lists. This week we help some new homeowners tackle the list for Fall.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: This home on Punch Bowl Drive in Westport is spacious in every way—except the kitchen. Stuck in its 1970's profile, it feels cramped and ready for a renovation. Mar redesigns this dated kitchen to meet the needs of a 21st-century family. Every bit of space is meticulously reworked with Casual Luxury for function and entertaining.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: The O'Kane family's patience is truly tested during a complete to-the-studs kitchen renovation. Homeowner Yvonne starts to get impatient to feed her family home cooked dinners. Added pressure: another fireplace to deal with, uneven floors, custom cabinets and an uncovered secret staircase. (All that plus a new puppy.) Thanks to Mar's 6 design principles this custom space is unique and stunning.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: It's 'Lost in Space' because somehow these home makeover clips never made it to Mar's! Bloopers Alert! Unseen Tips Alert! Inspired by viewer feedback, an entire show that includes highlights of the greatest gaffs, goofs and guffaws. Plus, some renovation and makeover projects never before aired.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Mar Jennings uses his 6 Casual Luxury design principles to transform a typical master bedroom, closets and bath into a boutique, hotel-style oasis. The O'Kanes are desperate to stop sleeping on their couches and get back into their master bedroom - but with a new cathedral ceiling installation, private deck installation, custom vanities, several setbacks and delays this renovation won't be easy.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Not only does Mar Jennings have the task of pleasing the adult O'Kanes, but he has to satisfy Teddy, a young man between childhood and young adulthood. AND try to make space in an already-small bedroom. Can it even be done? All while juggling two desk and office spaces-for Teddy and his parents. Mar's unexpected solutions for this kids bedroom and office makeover are truly Casual Luxury.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: More things that regular episodes just couldn't fit in, so we say they didn't quite make it to Mar's! Filled with some of Mar's favorite people and places that viewers wanted to see more of, and of course some delightfully hilarious bloopers, too. Plus, more renovation, makeover and design sMARt tips (like fixing uneven floors) and great locations. As always, Casual Luxury has no boundaries.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Mar is tasked with carving out two new basement rooms from storage space: a studio and laundry room. With Mar's 6 design principles, he must make dark and dank become bright and stunning. The homeowners expect to be wowed by these 'creative' and 'clean' space makeovers, but with pipes and beams in the way and unexpected challenges, this project might just push Mar over the edge.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Any home makeover has its ups and downs, but it's literally true with this staircase and foyer. Mar renovates the home's core areas, illustrating how to reclaim needed space, while creating impactful design that doesn't compete with adjacent rooms. An unexpected find-a gorgeous chandelier from a famous NY building-will be the perfect finishing touch if it makes it through the tricky installation.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: The Big Finale features Mar designing two useful, decorative his-and-hers structures to grace the pool area. Sheds become elevated design when form follows function--and include Mar's six core principles of Casual Luxury. Mar and his pup Violet find time for a much-needed minibreak to pet-friendly locations and shopping in NYC right before the final makeover reveal delights with surprise guests.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Season 2 premiere. Mar Jennings, known as America's Top Lifestyle Expert, is ready to renovate all the homes on his Westport, CT street. First up is a huge addition for neighbors Andrea and Bob's 1930's saltbox. Find out if Mar's new design team is up for the challenge. Part 1 of 2.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Season premiere part 2. Mar began a huge addition on the Nook, a 1930's saltbox, for his neighbors Bob and Andrea. But at the beginning of the dining and den walkthrough the homeowners were unreadable. Tune in to see if they will approve of Mar's progress and designs.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Master bedrooms and baths are things that dreams are made of until some nightmare hiccups happen. Turns out that visiting Broadway show sets provides unexpected inspiration. Amid creating the serene décor, Mar also finds a practical way to hide a litter box, and his team takes bets on which design his fans vote for.

programme.title: Moments in Time with Elia Locardi
programme.desc: The scenery changes to a lush countryside as Elia and his team travel to Malaysia. Local photographer, Yaman Ibrahim, shares his passion for street photography and takes Elia to some of his favorite spots in the historic old quarter.

programme.title: Moments in Time with Elia Locardi
programme.desc: Elia accidentally discovers the perfect spot for a sunrise shot of Kuala Lumpur, then meets-up with Grey Chow, a longtime friend and native. They venture outside the city to explore the Batu Caves, one of Malaysia's biggest attractions.

programme.title: Moments in Time with Elia Locardi
programme.desc: Elia shares advice on traveling with camera equipment and navigating Tokyo's subway system. He visits a peaceful garden then heads to the crowded Shibuya Crossing. Lastly, he visits the Photo History Museum and speaks with Sam Minami.

programme.title: Moments in Time with Elia Locardi
programme.desc: Elia explores the Odaiba waterfront with Giovanni Piliarvu, photographing the Rainbow Bridge. Then meets with Fujifilm's team to discuss the ever-changing market of photography. He ends with a memorable visit to the Robot Restaurant.

programme.title: Rudy Maxa's World
programme.desc: Follow Rudy to the elegant city of St. Petersburg, Russia.

programme.title: Smart Travels with Rudy Maxa
programme.desc: We can jet to Europe in a day and visit four cities in less than a week. It's great to take your time but sometimes you only have a day to visit. How can you be part of the international jet set and still get a taste of "old" Europe?

programme.title: Smart Travels with Rudy Maxa
programme.desc: Explore the world class European cities of London, Paris, Amsterdam, and Brussels. A European city bustles. It is a hub of commerce, the arts, energy, surprise, and international flavor. Where do you start if you have just 24-hours?

programme.title: Dream of Italy
programme.desc: In Perugia, we meet two women carrying on their family traditions of handmade stained glass and textiles, and we indulge in Baci candy. In Montefalco, Kathy tours the Tenuta Castelbuono winery, and enjoys a cooking lesson.

programme.title: Dream of Italy
programme.desc: Kathy McCabe invites you to experience the essence of Rome. Discover the Testaccio Market's incredible food, taste true Roman Cacio e Pepe and gelato, learn the beautiful craft of mosaics, and attend a grand ball at Palazzo Brancaccio.

programme.title: Smart Travels with Rudy Maxa
programme.desc: Rudy is in the city where all roads lead - Roma. We begin in ancient Rome - the Forum, Palatine Hill, Colosseum, and the Baths of Caracalla. Then, we explore the modern city of Rome with its ambient life in the streets, cafes, pizzerias and gelaterias.

programme.title: Smart Travels with Rudy Maxa
programme.desc: Europe's best kept secret, the Croatian coast of Dalmatia dazzles with stunning white stone cities and azure waters. We begin in Dubrovnik, then island hop up the coast, snorkeling, swimming, sunning and reveling in the scenery.

programme.title: Smart Travels with Rudy Maxa

Keeley forces them to help with his next planned heist: stealing gold from a wagon train led by Don Antonio Chaves. The men insinuate themselves with Chaves in preparation for the robbery, but when Will (Joel McCrea) falls in love with a widow (Arlene Dahl) traveling with Chaves, the three men must decide whether to continue the plan.

programme.title: The Sons of Katie Elder
programme.desc: Four ne'er-do-well sons reunite in their Texas hometown to attend their mother's funeral. Led by older brothers John (John Wayne), a gunfighter, and Tom (Dean Martin), a gambler, the four soon learn that their father gambled away the family ranch, which was the cause of his murder. The brothers decide to avenge their father's death and win back the ranch, a situation that quickly leads to trouble with the local sheriff and violent conflict with the rival Hastings clan.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: Stormchasing developed in Australia in the same period – the 1990s – as it developed in the American Midwest. In the first week of December – the height of storm season in Australia, we plan to travel with Jimmy Deguara, the most experienced of Australian stormchasers.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: Italy is one of the very few nations in the world that regularly gets three major extreme natural phenomena – volcanos, flooding, and avalanches. Eastern flanks of the volcano to slip by up to two metres, and many houses on the flanks of the volcano experienced structural damage. The eruption also completely destroyed the Rifugio Sapienza, on the southern flank of the volcano. The Rifugio was the site of a cable car station which had previously been destroyed in the 1983 eruption; both have now been rebuilt. The most recent eruption was in November 2006.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: It is time for “Angry Planet” to do a serious desert episode. What better place to do it than this fabled city in the middle of the mighty Sahara Desert.This almost mythical place really does exist. And it really is still possible to travel there by camel caravan, riding the infamous “Ships of the Desert”. Extreme heat, dehydration, possible sand storms, frigid night temperatures and desert predators are our potential companions for the trip.

programme.title: Five Little Chicks - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Five Little Chicks is great for nap time and for practicing counting down from five. Try singing along when it’s time for a nap.

programme.title: Rosey & The Rowdy Rustlers Tour Bus - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Rosey and The Rowdy Rustlers are coming to town to kick off their concert tour! But while driving out on the open road, a giant stray tumbleweed gets in the path of their tour bus, making a mighty mess! Rosey, Roxie, and Rude Jenny will have to take a detour to Carl’s Car Wash! With a quick mess assessment, Carl’s suds-sational car wash is ready to give Rosey and The Rowdy Rustlers’ vehicle a good scrub-a-dub! The band leaves as happy customers, and just in time for the concert!

programme.title: Who Took The Cookie? (Finny the Shark) - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Finny’s friends are playing a game of Who Took The Cookie From The Cookie Jar. Who do you think took the cookie? The seahorse, the starfish, the octopus, the walrus, the dolphin, or the crab? Let’s play and find out!

programme.title: Moolissa The Underwater Cow - Finny The Shark and more
programme.desc: Finny’s class finds out they have a new special friend coming for a visit, a cow named Moolisa! Being from land, it can be tricky for Moolisa to do everything that Finny’s friends do in the sea, but Moolisa will try her best to share in all the experiences of life in the ocean.

programme.title: Five Little Monsters Jumping On The Bed - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Here’s a silly and slightly spooky version of the classic kids’ rhyme, Five Little Monkeys, with monsters instead of monkeys. It’s a great song for practicing counting down from five, and for teaching little monsters (and children!) not to jump on the bed.

programme.title: Red Light Green Light - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Tobee learned all about traffic safety in school today! He learned how to walk in a crosswalk, to look both ways before crossing a street, and what the colors of a traffic light mean. That reminds Caitie of a song about stopping and going called “Red Light, Green Light.” Together, they sing and dance, stopping and starting again and again. Tobee and Caitie have a blast, and you can join the fun, too! Green light!

programme.title: Twinkle Twinkle Little Star - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: “Twinkle, twinkle little star. How I wonder what you are?” Lulu the Owl and Juno the Star become fast friends in this Super Simple version of one of the world’s most popular nursery rhymes.

programme.title: With My Heart - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Feel the warmth and learn the five senses in this heart warming Super Simple sensory song for kids. It’s also a perfect song to celebrate all parents. I can hear with my ears. I can see with my eyes.

programme.title: Weepy French Onion Soup - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Today, the Bumble Nums are going to make Weepy French Onion Soup. But the four weepy onions they need are VERY tricky to find. Will Humble, Grumble, and Stumble be able to spot all the onions and get back to the kitchen without crying too much? Watch and see!

programme.title: Seven Steps - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Counting to seven has never been more memorable and fun! Learn numbers with this simple counting song.

programme.title: Put On Your Shoes - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Here’s a Super Simple Song all about getting dressed! Sing along as we put on our shoes, jacket, hat, and scarf and then head out for a walk with the family.

programme.title: With My Heart - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Feel the warmth and learn the five senses in this heart warming Super Simple sensory song for kids. It’s also a perfect song to celebrate all parents. I can hear with my ears. I can see with my eyes.

programme.title: Let's Count To 100 - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: We are learning to count to 100 with Finny the Shark! This is a perfect numbers song for young kids and preschoolers! Let’s count to 100. Counting’s so much fun. Let’s count to 100. We’ll start from number 1.

programme.title: Let's Count To 100 - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: We are learning to count to 100 with Finny the Shark! This is a perfect numbers song for young kids and preschoolers! Let’s count to 100. Counting’s so much fun. Let’s count to 100. We’ll start from number 1.

programme.title: Goodbye A, Goodbye Z - Super Simple ABCs and more
programme.desc: We're saying, "Goodbye" to all of our friends in Alphabet Village. As the school day ends, take a walk through town, and bid farewell to all of the letters.

programme.title: Let's Go For A Drive! - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Get in on the fun and join Milo in his pretend car for a quick ride. Do you see the ducks crossing the road? How about people walking on the sidewalk? Wait, don't cross on a red light!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A boy in the Australian Outback wants to see a U.F.O. and meet an alien. He orders a mini satellite which unexpectedly leads to a rescue mission. Jett and Mira fly to Amphawa, Thailand, and deliver an enormous cake to a boy named Phra. They transport the cake on a raft through a floating market, but its fruit toppings attract the attention of some hungry Thai wildlife.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A girl who lives in the Netherlands asks for a tiny dress, a 'Dream Dress". When Jett and Bucky arrive, Nina tells them that she hopes it won't get taken, as a lot of things have been disappearing from her doll house lately. A girl on Easter Island asks for giant propeller caps for the Moai statues near her home. But the wind picks up and turns the Moai into head-helicopters.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A Scottish boy named Rooney asks the Super Wings to create a special mega-sized soccer uniform for his friend Jack, who is a tractor. Jack needs the uniform for an upcoming match of Tractor Soccer. A boy in Hawaii orders a special vacuum to clean up the garbage he's found in a bay where he rescued a young sea turtle. Before releasing the turtle, they discover a massive garbage island.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett's friend from Japan, Namiko, orders enormous sheets of origami paper. She and her friends use them for the Paper Rangers fan convention. But an animatronic of Paper Rangers' enemy Drac the Ripper goes haywire. Jett and Donnie deliver special tires to a boy named Gaspard in the Gobi Desert. Enjo gets overconfident with the new tires and accidentally startles some sheep.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: World Aircraft is flying through the Himalayas when an order comes in from a house near Mt Everest. Jett and Dizzy are sent to deliver some mega-sized art supplies to two brothers who want to make a mega-sized "Good Luck" card for their Dad. He's about to climb Mt. Everest! But when World Aircraft gets into trouble and crashes, it's going to take more than a Supercharge to save it!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A girl in Bogota asks for a spray-painting drone. When her jealous little dog steals the controls causing the drone to paint the town, they must get it back before he makes a huge mess. Jett and Donnie travel to Germany to bring a long wig to a girl named Kirsten. She wants to dress up as the famous princess, Rapunzel. Kirsten learns that it takes more than a wig to be a princess.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A girl in Switzerland orders an early release copy of a new book by a teenage adventure author. Discovering that the last chapter is missing, the Super Wings help her find the author. A boy named Pavel whose hobby is repairing watches, orders a special Super Wings wristwatch. Pavel takes them to see the Astronomy Clock in the Old Town Square, but they discover the old clock has stopped.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: When a boy and his grandmother board a luxury train in South Africa with self-inflating balloons, some animals cause the balloons to inflate, sending the train up into the sky. A girl named Kate from Bunbury, Australia, asks for a scanner that can go underwater. Jett and Mira learn that Kate needs it to analyze her favorite dolphin, Ninotchka. But Ninotchaka takes off after an undersea earthquake.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: On Halloween, a boy in California orders his own fully-functional Super Wings costume. But a haunted house scares Donnie haywire and he gets a giant pumpkin decoration stuck on his head! Jett and Paul fly to Cairo, where a girl has ordered a special item to help her lead a tour of the Cairo Museum for her friends. But the girl's little brother accidentally brings the mummies and sphinx to life!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Two boys who are pals that live in Greece, are huge mythology fans. They ask for a card game with mythological creatures that are holograms. When the creatures get too big, they threaten to destroy a town. A boy who loves everything about wizards wants to be a real wizard with a real magic wand. The closest Storm can come to making one is a toy wand with some Supercharge energy stored in it.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A girl in Moscow with a rock collection orders a special box with compartments labeled for the coolest rocks ever. When she discovers that her collection doesn't have the last three rocks needed to complete the box, the Superwings must help her get them by going on a trip into space, because the rocks needed are space rocks!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A Dad in Brazil orders a special explorer's outfit for his son. A huge wave rushes up the Amazon River and threatens the river animals and a village. A girl living in Dezhou China, is excited that she and her parents are hosting the Chinese New Year celebrations for the whole family. But traffic jams on land, sea, and air cause trouble for all the family members from other provinces.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A girl, Akaja, asks the Super Wings for molds that can make multi-colored ice blocks. She wants the blocks to build an igloo that holds a playground that holds a playground. A boy named Nuno in Porto, Portugal orders a large, clear, unbreakable bottle. He wants to build a model boat in a bottle. When the SWs and he shrink down to build the boat, the bottle is accidentally knocked out of a window.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A girl in Kuala Lumpur orders a special swing for the new baby orangutan to play on. But a mishap makes the orangutan become king-sized and it escapes from the zoo. A girl in Mexico named Daniela orders some cat treats shaped like marigolds. Jett and Astra learn that Daniela wants to use the treats to attract the attention of her recently deceased cat, Sanchez, for 'Day of The Dead.'

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A girl named Ruma in Bangladesh orders a Table Top Cricket Game with moving players. Ruma and her friends like the game, but want to play a real game of cricket. A boy from France orders a machine that can make nutritious cotton candy out of vegetables. It's for a world culture fair being held at the boy's school. The boy puts too many vegetables into the machine creating a catastrophe!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: World Aircraft returns to World Airport for a special day of races in front of the Super Wings' biggest fans - the children of the world! The contestants are in for a test when a surprise challenger shows up. A girl living in Michoacán, Mexico orders a pair of costume butterfly wings and a flower crown. She hopes the butterflies will think she's a butterfly too and will land on her flower crown.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett delivers a Pasta Making Machine to a boy in Pisa, Italy. The machine is to help him win a contest for the biggest noodle in town. Jett delivers a Spinning Top to a boy, Kenta, in Sapporo, Japan! Kenta wants to use the top to enter the Spinning Top Competition at the local Snow Festival. Things go well until Golden Boy enters the competition, by making himself into a spinning top.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Jet Pet join a girl and her father to look for a lost tiger kitten's mother in the jungle of Madhya Pradesh, India - the same jungle from the girl's favorite book, "The Jungle Book" by Rudyard Kipling. Jett delivers drone wings to a girl, Lea, in Geneva. They are for her car, Leo, who now can fly, a skill that he hopes will win him the "Best Car of the Year" award at a local Motor show.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: With the Dolittle, and other Baby programs under their belts, the tots who follow Julie Aigner-Clark's Baby Einstein series will sidle up to Baby Newton expecting a similar exploration of building blocks to brilliance, but this time there's a wrinkle or two. Animation makes its first appearance, and parents steeped in keep-it-simple culture may object to the springy "I Know My Shapes" song, a departure from classical-only video predecessors.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Hop aboard to see vehicles that travel by land, water and sky! See real-life images of cars, boats and planes in this on-the-go adventure that features cameos from some of your favorite Baby Einstein characters, all while listening to music from around the world.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Meet fascinating creatures from across the globe and learn to run, hop, and crawl like your favorite animal friends. Dance and sing along to discover animal names and the native habitats of the world's most unique creatures. See real-life images of lions and zebras on the African savannah, then learn animal names through repetition and narration, or simply watch your favorite animals play!

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Enjoy the best of summer, autumn and winter with Baby Einstein! Discover the familiar sights and sounds of the different seasons. Learn names of common objects associated with each through repetition and narration and see engaging images of whimsical toys that celebrate each. Discover the magic of each season from falling leaves, rolling beach balls and even snowmen.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As their communication skills continue to develop, little ones begin to recognize common words and may point to objects or pictures when these items are named. Baby Wordsworth playfully taps into your little one's natural curiosity and introduces 30 words from around the home -- both spoken and in sign language. This engaging program presents little ones with familiar objects in the kitchen, playroom, yard and more -- from the living room window to their favorite teddy bear.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Ocean Explorers
programme.desc: Cal, Neptune, Opus, Dean, Tinker and others learn about the flora and fauna of the ocean through STEAM-based principles and real-life footage.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Get moving with Baby Einstein! Real, live-action footage and classical melodies engage young viewers as they discover simple words related to movement. Enjoy encouraging melodies and live-action footage of babies and animals as you learn and move alongside your favorite Baby Einstein friends. Dance along as you watch real, live action footage of animals, children, and toys.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Babies love animals, and now you can bring animals from the seven continents home to your baby. Meet dozens of exotic animals including Asian pandas, Australian kangaroos and Arctic snow monkeys...all filmed in their beautiful, natural habitats. Rhythms and songs inspired by faraway lands have been orchestrated especially for little ears and encourage your baby to sing, dance and play to the world beats. You'll laugh together at the funny puppet shows featuring moms and their baby animals.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As babies grow and change, so does the landscape around them. From fiery red leaves to falling snowflakes; from flowers in bloom to fireworks in July, every season of the year has its own special magic just waiting to be discovered.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Learn about the numbers 1,2,3,4 & 5 through rhyme, real-life footage, counting, stories, and repetition - accompanied by classical music and real world imagery.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones become better acquainted with the world, they grow curious about animals of all kinds, beyond just the family pet. And as their communication skills continue to develop, they enjoy imitating animal sounds as well. Baby Noah Animal Expedition takes your little explorer across the globe to meet animals -- two-by-two -- from the savannah, the rainforest and tropics, the Outback, the ocean and the Polar Regions.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: From the moment babies are born, they use their developing senses to discover the world. In terms of sight, bold patterns in contrasting colors or black and white are initially preferred. As for sounds, Mother s gentle voice, in any language, is music to baby's ears. Language Nursery: Voices From Many Lands acquaints your baby with the sounds of foreign language by presenting delightful, visually stimulating images accompanied by spoken passages, natural sounds and music.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Early on in babies' development, they begin to recognize primary colors such as blue, red, and yellow. And all the while, they're also processing an infinite variety of new sights and sounds. Think of the joy you'll experience as you introduce your little one to beautiful music and imagery with Baby Beethoven Symphony of Fun.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: World Music introduces you and your little one to beautiful sounds from every continent, bringing a fresh, new experience of music and culture. Sing, dance and play to exotic rhythms that open the door to an appreciation of different cultures from around the world.

programme.title: Five Little Penguins + More Kids Songs from Kids TV
programme.desc: The Five Little Penguins have been jumping in the snow. What's going to happen when one falls down?

programme.title: Three Little Pigs & More Kids Stories - Super Supremes
programme.desc: This fun collection of nursery rhymes features stories starring the superhero family The Super Supremes!

programme.title: Five Little Ducks Nursery Rhymes & Kids Songs - Boom Buddies
programme.desc: Five Little Duck, Bingo The Dog, Incy Wincy Spider and many more Boom Buddies rhymes! Happy singing!

programme.title: Nursery Rhymes for Children + More Kids Song - Jelly Bears
programme.desc: Join Jelly Bears as they dance to preschool rhymes like Five Little Teddy Bears, Johny Johny and more!

programme.title: Super Supremes Old Macdonald & More Kids Songs
programme.desc: Kids! Are you ready to go round the Farmees town? Hop on-board this Red Wheels On The Bus to enjoy your fun learning ride.

programme.title: Booya Non-Stop
programme.desc: Follow the hilarious adventures of five adorable kooky monsters who live under a kid's bed. Living under the bed is easy and kind of safe but these adorable characters are impulsive, curious, and always ready for fun adventures. With a playful dog who is bent on messing up their plans, the group keeps venturing out, finding ways to dodge the snoopy sniffy, creating chaos and laughter around them.

programme.title: Super Supremes Wheels On The Bus & More Popular Kids Song
programme.desc: Do you kids know Wheels on the Bus? Learn this classic nursery rhyme with The Supremes, plus many other fun songs and dances.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Robotnik has his eyes set upon Queen Aleena's crown jewel, but Sonic, Manic and Sonia plan to retrieve the gem before Robotnik.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Sonic, Manic and Sonia find an abandoned baby in an alley and begin to look for the baby's parents. Manic becomes very fond of the child.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Robotnik announces that he will marry Queen Aleena in a plot to become the king of Mobius.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: A captain of the Queen's guard is captured before he can reach the secret rendezvous where he was to meet Queen Aleena.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Sonia discovers that the watch she received from her friend is actually a monitoring device for the Robotnik Intelligence Agency.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: During a mission to one of Robotnik's factories, Sally is taken prisoner by Robotnik and Snively, who construct a roboti-sized "Sally" clone, complete with Sally's personality, and mannerisms. The double tricks Sonic into rescuing "Sally".

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic and the Freedom Fighters journey to Robotnik's lab in hopes of deciphering his roboti-sizer system. While underground, Sonic spots his now roboti-sized Uncle Chuck aboard a Badlands bound mine-train and devises a plan for his rescue.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sally, Rotor, Bunnie and Antoine take off in a homemade airplane, bound for an ancient city hidden deep in the Great Mountains, while Sonic follows via his favorite transportation, his sneakers.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic is hired into an obvious trap when a frustrated Robotnik decides to appeal to the Hedgehog's ego and announces a "race to determine the fastest on Mobius".

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic conquers Robotnik's latest evil - a vehicle designated to locate and destroy living tissue matter - during a solo mission to the Badlands. Upon Sonic's return, the Freedom Fighters honor him with a victory party.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Robotnik launches a spy-orb that will uncover the Freedom Fighter's Knothole location, Sonic and Robot launch themselves up to the orbiting spy-sphere aboard a home-made rocket powered by Robotnik's discarded booster engine.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Sally uncovers Robotnik's plan to seed the cloud with toxic rain, they attempt a daring mission to sabotage the dirigible-like craft being transported by land vehicles. Mission fails when Sally, Bunnie and Antoine are captured.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Sonic and The Freedom Fighters take out Robotnik's main computer, they're trapped by Swat-Bots in an underground tunnel, only to be rescued by a handsome teenager named Griff. Griff takes them deep beneath the Big City.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Sonia is invited to a debutante ball, but before she can be officially announced to society, she and her brothers, Manic and Sonic have an important mission.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Rumor has it that Queen Aleena's journals are buried in the city of Tashistan. Sonic and his siblings search the city for the scrolls.

programme.title: Sonic Underground

programme.desc: For his sixth birthday, Mark Whitley, was given a hammer and toolbox. Now an award-winning furniture maker in Smiths Grove, Kentucky, Mark and host Eric Gorges make a table together.

programme.title: Grandpa's War Story Goes Viral
programme.desc: For Alaskan teenager Henry De Hoop, social media platforms such as Facebook have provided a unique opportunity to share the World War II story of his grandfather, James (Jim) Schmidt, with a global audience. Narrated by Liev Schreiber, GRANDPAS WAR STORY GOES VIRAL shares the remarkable account of Jims World War II service and military career, as well as Henrys efforts to generate interest in his grandfathers war journey and launch a letter-writing campaign on his behalf.Like 16 million others of his generation, Jim responded to the national call to duty following the bombing of Pearl Harbor in 1941. The twist in the story? Jim was only 14 when he enlisted, and the eighth-grade Californian left home without telling his parents. After being wounded in the invasion of Sicily, Jims age was eventually revealed and he was sent home. Not to be deterred, Jim would do stints in both the U.S. Navy and Merchant Marines before finally reaching 18 and officially rejoining the Army. Grandson Henry marveled that Jim joined the war effort at the same age he was when he first shared Jim's story on social media.GRANDPAS WAR JOURNEY GOES VIRAL charts a story of service that began in a movie theater in 1942 where the film Parachute Battalion grabbed a boys attention and ends in the jungles of Vietnam more than two decades later.
